[EXO 17:7]; 3 • to live, dwell in/at. M mini a biɛhigu pala yilinga ka m pii waa ni. Our relationship is not like the a jingle; that I pick up at the dancing place. It is not right that we should squat in the; bush or defecate carelessly in our living space. [DB-Hygiene.009] Ant: ka 4 .; 4 • to live, be alive. O be o nyɛvil' ni./ O be ni He is alive. Bɛ ni ti leei bɛ ni ŋme tɔbu di shɛba ka be. They will; become survivors from the war. [JER 38:2] Lit: ... those that they defeat in war but they are (kept) alive. If your father and mother are still; alive you do whatever you want and if they have died, then you allow the bath room to become dirty.; [Prov. 2]; biɛhigu ti ku niŋ asama tin ya life won't be easy for you. Colloc: be biɛhigu ‘to live a life, have a lifestyle’. Bɛ bela ʒiri; biɛhigu o sani. They live a deceitful life with him. Note: sn 2 Sim: jifa.; 1; 2 • "an old rag" (IM). Colloc: poora biŋkpiŋ ‘a powder-puff’; Syn: binchɛrili. See: bini; 'kpiŋ.; biŋkpiiŋa Pl: biŋkpiinsi. n. a small thing. Note: ?? adj kpiiŋa See: bini.; biŋmaa Pl: biŋmahi. n. diarrhœa, watery stool. Sim: binsaa. 1 1; bira See main entry: birili . Note: sn 2 rosella.; 2 2; birapu n. a bribe. Bɛ zaa yuri birapu dibu ni faɣiri dibu. They all love getting bribes and 'presents'. [ISA 1:23] Bɛn diri; zalinsi nyɛla bɛn bɔri yɛla tiri bɛ yiŋnima, amaa bɛn je birapu dibu ni be. Dishonest money brings grief to the; whole family, but those who hate bribes will live. [PRO 15:27] birapu dibu nyɛla din chɛ ka baŋda nina bi ne a; bribe blinds the eyes of the wise. [DEU 16:19] Sim: faɣiri ‘loot, free gift’. From: (Eng.)

birigi Variant: birgi. Pl: birigira. Pl: biriginsim. Variant: birigibu; briginsim. [EXO 34:7 and 44x] Ti niŋ lala .. dindina; ti birigiya. As we acted like that ... indeed, we were in error. [2CH 19:10]; v. 1 • to err, deviate, be deceived. N kul dolila a soya viɛnyɛliŋga ka na ʒi m-birigi. I just follow your ways well; and have never deviated. [PSA 17:5] Sim: barigi 1 . Note: sn 1 Sim: yirigi. Note: sn 1 Sim: chirim. Note: sn 1 Sim: tum 3 .; 2 • to deceive, cause sb. to err. Miriya ka yi ti chɛ ka so birigi ya Mind you don't let anyone deceive you. [LUK 21:8]; Biriginsim din ni kpɛhi nira alahichi ni ku chɛ Deception which brings a person into sin is unavoidable. [MAT; 18:7] ninvuɣ’ yɔhindiba bɛn zaŋ yɛnkiŋkabina soli birigi niriba la ni zaŋ yɔhiŋgu wuhibu 'deceitful people,; who lead others into error by the tricks they invent'. [EPH 4:4] Sim: chirim. Note: sn 2; 3 • to branch off, give way, step aside, take short cut. Yawɛ ni daa nya ka Musa birigi kanna ni o ti nya li maa; when the Lord saw that Moses had turned aside to see it. [EXO 3:4] Ti birigi n-kpe. We turned aside and went in.; [2CH 6:37,39] Yi yi ti birigi kpa nudirigu polo bee nuzaa polo ka chɛ soli If you turn aside to the right or the left; and leave the path. [ISA 30:21] Sim: ŋmaligi ‘turn, return’.; 4 • to scandalize, offend sb. Bɛ daa bi tum m-birigi ti Duuma Naawuni. They did not act so as to offend against; our Lord God. (bɛ) daa ti tum birigi bɛ dan’ Ijipti naa maa (they) had offended against their master the king of; Egypt.

biyola Pl: biyoliba. n. 1 • a child-nurse, babysitter, one who attends babies. bɔba. n. track, footprint. any continuous visible track, as of an animal or bicycle

(a) zahindi n napɔna bɔba (you) are watching every step I take. [JOB; 13:27] kaman binyɛrigu kpabbu bɔba la ... kaman bɛ ni zaŋ binshɛli dihi yaɣiri zuɣu ka yihi li ka di bɔbili; doya shɛm la like the folds of a garment ... like when they take something and press it in clay and take it away and; its imprint remains. [JOB 38:14] Lit: the traces of the folding of a garment. O ŋmɛri bɔbili din nyaɣisira pari kom; zuɣu o nyaaŋa. He leaves a shining track on top of the water behind him. [JOB 41:32] Waɣi so ŋun mali logu yi; dim nira, a ni nya ka o nyin kara maa bɔba doya. If a poisonous snake bites somebody, you will see the marks; of his fangs there. [DB snake 2.018] pa ni man’ ti nya o nuhi ni kpahi bɔba la, ka zaŋ n nubil’ bɔbili kpahi maa; bɔba maa ni 'Unless I see the nail marks in his hands and put my finger where the nails were'. bɔɣi Pl: bɔɣira. v. 1 • to split (intransitive). tiŋgbani daa dam ka tampima bɔɣi the earth quaked and the rocks split.; [MAT 27:51] Tiŋgbani daa bɔɣiya m-mum Datan The earth split open and buried Dathan. 2 1; bɔɣisapinkɔbili Variant: bɔɣusapinkɔbili. Pl: bɔɣisapinkɔba. n. shoulderblade. See: bɔɣisapiŋ; kɔbili .; 2; bɔɣisapiŋ Variant: bɔɣisapiuŋ; bɔɣusapim; bɔɣusapiŋ. Pl: bɔɣisapima. n. shoulder. "withers" (of horse) (IM). n naan; zaŋ li buɣi m bɔɣisapiŋ zuɣu I would carry it on my shoulder. [JOB 31:36] O ni yuri so ʒila o bɔɣisapima; sunsuuni. the one the LORD loves rests between his shoulders. [DEU 33:12] Bɛ daa zuɣisi bɛ bɔɣisapima ka niŋ; tipawumli They shrugged(?) their shoulders and disobeyed. [NEH 9:29] Colloc: bɔɣisapiŋ kɔbili ‘shoulder blade’.; dindina nyin’ chɛ ka m bɔɣisapiŋ kɔbili kparigi then you should cause my shoulderblade to be dislocated. [(1) ISA 49:22] Musa bɔɣu daa ti ja; And Moses shoulder got tired (from holding up his staff). [EXO 17:12] Bɔɣu m-beni ka lɛhu paya. The axe rests on; the shoulder so long as the shoulder is there to support it. bɔɣu 2 Pl: bɔri. n. a yam farm. "To" bi naara bɔɣu. Saying “Okay” does not finish the yams on the yam farm. [Prov. 2649]; Zawurugu zuɣu ka mani zaŋ m bɔɣu pari buŋgu. It is because of (the itching that I get from) the husks of millet; that I plant sesame in succession on my yam farm. [Prov. 2913] Sim: batandali ‘year after yams’; kukɔɣu ‘second; year after yams’; kaɣsɔɣɔ ‘third year after yams’; gbansɔɣu ‘fourth year after yams’. Etym: ("valley") OV1 *BO ;

words.grammar: ka bi ti li ŋmanila saa ni yi ku ka bi mi la. 'Like clouds and wind without rain is a man who boasts of gifts he; does not give.'. [PRO 25:14] Colloc: saa kura ‘a storm is threatening’. Ninvuɣ’ so ŋun nyu bara ni o ni ti pini ka; 303; bi ti li ŋmanila saa ni yi ku ka bi mi la. A man who boasts of gifts he does not give is like rain that threatens but; never falls. [PRO 25:14] Syn: vu. Note: sn 2 “... saa vura.” ... Di daa bi yuui ka saa ku n-zibigi, ka pɔhim gbaai; ʒiɛbu, ka sa’ kurili dii piligi mibu. " ... it's going to rain." ... Not long afterwards the sky loured and grew dark,; and the wind began to blow, and a torrential rain started to fall. [1KI 18:41...45] Syn: ŋari 1 . Note: sn 4; ku Pl: kura. v. 1 • to satisfy a need. Yi diri bindira, amaa ka di bi kuri ya kum; ka nyuri binnyura, amaa ka di bi; 3; kuri ya di kɔre. Yi yɛri situra, amaa ka di bi kuri ya wari You eat food but it doesn't satisfy your hunger, you; drink drinks, but they don't satisfy your need for drink. You wear clothes, but they don't keep you from the cold.; [HAG 1:6] Colloc: ku 3 buchi ‘to serve a useful purpose; to be profitable’. M mini kum kul chɛ tab'la kpe ni kpe,; ka buchi bɔ ka kpamdi lan yɛn ku ma? I'm only a step away from death and what further use is my seniority to; me? [GEN 25:32] Lit: I and death just are apart from each other here and here .... di bi ku buchi shɛli it became; useless (? doesn't fulfil any purpose). Gbaamiya saawara, amaa di ku ku buchi. Take counsel, but it won't; succeed. [ISA 8:10] Colloc: ku 3 bukaata ‘to serve a useful purpose, fill a long-felt want’. machɛli kuriti bidibbini; din ni ku di bukaata the blacksmith forges a weapon fit for its work. [ISA 54:16] o daa zaɣisila bukaata kam; kubu he refused to do anything useful. [DB-Chiefships.037] Sim: naɣi 3 . o daa zaɣisila bukaata kam kubu, ka daa; naɣi kuli gurila soya n-fari niriba buni n-dira he refused to do anything useful but just ambushed the paths and; robbed people of their possessions. words.grammar: 1 2 1; kpaɣito excl. Have at you! - expression used to provoke a match or quarrel.; kpaɣiwurigu n. disease [type] "with rashes on your skin" (FK). Syn: wurigu. Note: sn 3 See: kpaɣa .; 1; kpaɣiyaɣu n. disease [type], "pains around waist during menstruation" (FK). See: kpaɣa .; 1; kpaɣiʒiɛɣu Variant: kpaɣiʒiɛɣu; kpaɣuʒiɛɣu. Pl: kpaɣiʒiɛri. n. malaria, fever. Note: 'red (serious') fever: "you vomit; yellowish substances" (FK) Daŋ zaŋ kpaɣiʒeɣu mini kpariyuri n-chaŋ ashibiti Waste no time in taking malaria; and roundworm/hookworm to the hospital. [DB AIDS 01.031] Kom ni ʒiri dɔri' shɛŋa n-nyɛ, nyɛrifu mini binsaa ni; pumahigu ni kpariyuri ni kpaɣuʒiɛɣu. Some water-borne diseases are guineaworm, an diarrhœa and dysentery; and hookworms and fever. [DB-Hygiene.034] duunsi mi yi dim ti, di tiri ti kpaɣu ʒiɛɣu (maleeriya) and if the; mosquitoes bite us, it gives us red-fever (malaria). [DB.856] Gen: kpaɣa; Spec: kpaɣipiɛliga. See: kpaɣa; 'ʒiɛɣu 2 .

words.grammar: kpambala Pl: kpambaliba. n. a court elder, senior elder, courtier. N dan’ naa, n ni leeg’ a kpambala. Kaman n ni yi; kul nyɛ a ba kpambala shɛm, lala ka n yɛn nyɛ a gba kpambala Your majesty, I will become your counsellor.; As I have been your father's counsellor, so I will be your counsellor too. [2SA 15:34] a zaŋ kpariti maa mini wɔhu; maa ti a kpambaliba ni yino n-chɛ ka o zaŋ li ti bɔbi (o) get the robes and horse and give them to one of your; senior elders so that he should use them to equip (him). [EST 6:9] Firawuna kpambaliba ni daa nya o, bɛ daa paɣ’; o when Pharaoh's courtiers saw her, they praised her. [GEN 12:15] a pii kpambal’ shɛb’ ka bɛ su tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ tuma; fukumsi you choose some officials who will be in charge of the functioning of the country. [GEN 41:34]

Di lan viɛli pam ni a nyuri kpaazaa. It is also good to drink ORS. [DB AIDS 04.023] Di yi lee; ka kpaazaa kani a ni tooi bɔ yɛlim, sichiri n’lɔhibi kom ni. If there is no ORS, you can get salt and sugar and; dissolve them in water. [DB AIDS 05.002] Note: ?? see examples Sim: yalim.; kpe Pl: kpɛra. v. 1 • to enter, go into. Ka o daa kpe vokabili puuni zoli maa zuɣu nti gbe nimaani. And he went into; 1; a cave on the mountain and spent the night there. [1KI 19:9] Piɛm yi na bi kpe, di ŋoobu bi to. If an arrow has not; yet entered (deeply), it is not difficult to pull it out. [Prov 2224] Colloc: kpe 1 loori/ŋariŋ n-chaŋ ‘to board, 'get in a; 337; vehicle/boat and go'’; Colloc, kpe X yuli ‘to seek X’s protection’. Bɛ daa chaŋ ti kpɛla Firawuna yuli they; 1 1; 'look for help to Pharaoh's protection'. [ISA 30:2] Colloc: kpe 1 mɔɣu/yɔɣu 2 ‘go astray; 'go into the bush'’; kpe 1; wurigadaa ‘"to run away, run in different directions" (IM)’; kɔŋa kpe X ‘'leprosy' infects X’. Kɔŋa kpɛr’ a ka; 1 1; a yɛli ni ŋamli. Leprosy attacks you, but you say that it is a skin rash. [Prov. 1274] Colloc: kpe 1 guŋgɔŋ ‘"to; undertake a gun salute during a funeral, shoot guns at a funeral one after the other" (IM)’; kpe saha ‘"(of the; 1; night) to be advanced" (IM)’; dabiɛm kpe X ‘X is filled with fear’. M birimmi zaa ka dabiɛm kpe ma. I am in; 1; a panic and filled with fear. [ISA 21:4] Colloc: simdi kpe 1 X (mi)ni Y sunsuuni ‘X and Y are reconciled’. simdi; yipa kpe ti mini Naawuni sunsuuni o Bia la kum zuɣu we are now reconciled to God by the death of his Son.; [ROM 5:10] Colloc: X kɔre kpe 1 Y ‘1) (Y is a thing) X desires Y’. N daa nyala Shiina binyɛr’ suŋ ... ka n kɔre kpe; di ni I saw a fine Shinar coat ... and I desired it. [JOS 7:21] N kɔre n-kpe m mabia Absalom tizɔpaɣ’ Tama ni. 'I'm; in love with Tamar, my brother Absalom's sister.'. [2SA 13:4] Colloc: kpe 1 yibilisi ‘to be mad, go mad’; kpe 1; yinyaari ‘to become mad’; kpe gungɔŋ ‘"to shoot guns at a funeral one after the other"’; kpe X yuli ‘to seek; 1 1 1; X's protection’; kpe X gaɣili ni ‘to fall into X's trap’; kpe X kpaɣa ni ‘to incur X's displeasure’; kpe yoli; 1 1 1 1 1 3

lai n. 1 • a queue, 'line' [Am.] Syn: foli. From: (Eng.) Note: "line"; 2 • barracks. From: (Eng.) Note: "lines", military sense (esp. horse lines'); lakɔrigu Note: ?? Pl: lakɔriti. n. a clay bowl [type]. Yaɣimɛriba ni mɛri neen’ shɛŋa m-bɔŋɔ furlanima, lakɔriti, yaɣiri; jinlaa, ʒɛbahisi, nɔsaya mini neen’ shɛŋa din kam zaa pahi. These are the things that potters mould: lamps,; ()-bowls, clay ablution-watervessels, soup-pots, fowls' drinking-pots and all sorts of other things.; [DB-Trades.018]

lali Note: ?? cf. laali1 Pl: lalinda. v. 1 • to glide, hover, float in the air.; 2 • to swing from, dangle. words.dagbani: bɔ 2 zuɣu 2 ‘why>’. Bihili mɔɣisibu waa-waayili ni tooi naai paɣi bihim? Bɔ zuɣu?
words.grammar: Can frequent suckling purify the milk supply? Why? [Bihili MǤǳisibu 9.013-4] Syn: dama, domi; Sim: dinzuɣu; ‘therefore’.; 3 • for. Note: ?? the various 'senses' are different English translatons, not distinct in Dagbani Colloc: wɔɣilim zuɣu; 1 2; ‘lengthwise, along the length’.; 4 • on, upon, on top of. Tia din bɔri yuli dina n-zaani zoli zuɣu. The tree that wants to be famous grows on top; of a hill. [Prov. 2576] Wabili yi ti tam tia zuɣu, bɛ duhi o mi. If a lame person is sitting in a tree, others have lifted; him up and put him there. [Prov. 2688] kɔhi X zuɣu ‘sell to X’. Note: on top (‘head’ zuǳu1 ) of (see gbinni - note); 'zuɣu Pl: 'zuɣuri. adj. head, chief, (most) important. Colloc: tin'zuɣu ‘capital city’. Susa tin’ shɛli din daa nyɛ tin’; 3; zuɣu la ni Susa, the city which was the capital. [NEH 1:1] Colloc: (ninvuɣ'zuɣu/)ninvuɣ'zuɣuri. Note: ?? pl. only; ninvuɣ’ zuɣuri tim bɛ tumtumdiba important people send their servants. [JER 14:3] Colloc: na' zuɣu ‘head chief,; 790
